module SceneManager
  def self.call_time_choice(choiceID)
    $game_system.choiceID=choiceID
    #BattleManager.set_battle_hold if(SceneManager.scene_is?(Scene_Battle))
    snap_timechoice_bitmap
    call(Scene_TimeChoice)
  end
  def self.scene_class_equ(scene,scene_class)
    scene.instance_of?(scene_class)
  end
  
  def self.snap_timechoice_bitmap
    @time_choice_bitmap=Graphics.snap_to_bitmap
  end
  def self.scene_timechoice_bitmap
    @time_choice_bitmap
  end
end

module Cache
  def self.choice(filename)
    load_bitmap("Graphics/Pictures/", filename)
  end
end

class Game_Interpreter
  def command_355
    script = @list[@index].parameters[0] + "\n"
    while next_event_code == 655
      @index += 1
      script += @list[@index].parameters[0] + "\n"
    end
    last_scene=SceneManager.scene
    eval(script)
    if(script=~/SceneManager.call_time_choice.*/)
      if(!SceneManager.scene_class_equ(last_scene,Scene_Battle))
      $game_message.busy=true
      wait_for_message
      end
    end
  end
end

class Game_Message
  attr_accessor :busy
  
  def busy?
    has_text? || choice? || num_input? || item_choice? ||@busy
  end
  
  def initialize
    clear
    @visible = false
    @busy=false
  end 
end
============= SCRIPT 134: Script Block Eval =============
class Game_Interpreter
  def command_355
    script = @list[@index].parameters[0] + "\n"
    while next_event_code == 655
      @index += 1
      script += @list[@index].parameters[0] + "\n"
    end
    last_scene=SceneManager.scene
    eval(script)
    if(script=~/SceneManager.call_time_choice.*/)
      if(!SceneManager.scene_class_equ(last_scene,Scene_Battle))
      $game_message.busy=true
      wait_for_message
      end
    end
  end
end
============= SCRIPT 135:  =============

============= SCRIPT 136: Sylph - Scheduler =============
#==============================================================================
# ** Scheduler
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Version:  1.0.1
# Date:     27/07/2020
# Author:   SylphPhoenix
#
# This utility streamlines the scheduling of processes either through one-time
# delayed tasks or repeated actions with a fixed interval between executions.
#
# It revolves around the idea of a Task object, though the initialization of
# those objects is usually wrapped with a function because of how many
# parameters exist for it.
#
# Scheduling is attached to screen updates, meaning scheduled tasks will occur
# regardless of game state.
#
#==============================================================================

#==================================+
# Injects scheduler initialization |
#==================================+
module DataManager
  class << self
    alias original_create_game_objects create_game_objects
    def create_game_objects
      original_create_game_objects
      $scheduler = Scheduler.new if $scheduler.nil?
    end
  end
end

#===============================================+
# Injects scheduler update into screen updates. |
#===============================================+
class Game_Screen
  alias original_update update
  def update
    original_update
    $scheduler.update
  end
end


# ============================================================================+
# * Task
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
#
# This is an actual task that will be executed on the scheduler.
#=============================================================================+
class Task

  # ==========================================================================+
  # Constructor:
  # => frames: The number of frames between/until execution. There are 60fps.
  # => times: The number of times this will execute. For one-time tasks, 1.
  # => name: The name of the task so that it can be unscheduled arbitrarily.
  # => start_immediately: On repeated tasks, true if it should skip first delay.
  # => end_condition: A lambda with a task parameter executed before every task
  #                   execution. Returning true within the lambda will cause the
  #                   task to end. E.g. ->(t) { t.times_executed > @something }
  # => action: The code to execute, as a code block with the task as a
  #            parameter, e.g. ->(t) { do_steam_puff } where t is this task.
  # ==========================================================================+
  attr_accessor :name
  
  def initialize(frames, times, name, start_immediately, end_condition, action)
    @frames = frames
    @times = times
    @name = name
    @end_condition = end_condition

    # Handling nil is annoying, replace it with a lambda that always gives false
    if @end_condition.nil?
      @end_condition = ->(task) { false }
    elsif @end_condition.arity == 0
      # Normalize lambda so @end_condition always has one argument.
      @end_condition = ->(task) { end_condition.call }
    end

    if action.arity == 0
      # Normalize lambda so @action always has one argument.
      @action = ->(task) { action.call }
    else
      @action = action
    end

    reset_fiber

    # When starting immediately the delay will be zero.
    @delay = 0
    @delay = frames unless start_immediately
    @times_executed = 0
    @cancelled = false
  end

  # Returns the number of times this task has executed so far.
  def times_executed
    @times_executed
  end

  # Resets the @fiber definition using @action
  def reset_fiber
    @fiber = Fiber.new do
      @fiber_started = true
      @action.call(self)
      @fiber_finished = true
    end
    @fiber_started = false
    @fiber_finished = false
  end

  def start_fiber
    @fiber.resume
  end

  # Executed every frame.
  #   Returns true if the task should continue, otherwise false.
  def should_continue
    # Cancelling or satisfying the end_condition will short circuit the task.
    if @cancelled == true || @end_condition.call(self) == true
      return false
    end

    if @fiber_started == true
      @fiber.resume
    else
      @delay = @delay - 1
      if @delay <= 0
        @fiber.resume
      end
    end

    if @fiber_finished == true
      @times_executed = @times_executed + 1
      @delay = @frames
      if @times_executed >= @times
        return false
      else
        reset_fiber
      end
    end

    return true
  end

  def handle_tick

  end

  # Cancels the task so that the next time it tries to run, it is removed.
  def cancel
    @cancelled = true
  end

  # Returns @cancelled
  def cancelled
    @cancelled
  end
end

# ============================================================================+
# * Scheduler
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
#
# Scheduler instance stored under $scheduler. This is the object to use for
# scheduling an action to occur in parallel.
#
# I've taken the liberty of adding a handful of 'shortcut' functions such as
# 'after' and 'every' which are just calls to 'add_task' but with easier to
# understand parameters.
#
# Feel free to add more functions to this class to make your life easier.
# ============================================================================+
class Scheduler
  def initialize
    p "Initializing scheduler!"
    @tasks = [];
  end

  # =================================================================+
  # Schedules an action to occur once, after the given number of frames.
  #
  # Parameters:
  # => frames: The number of frames until this will occur. 60fps.
  # => action: A lambda block containing the action that will occur.
  # =================================================================+
  def after(frames, action)
    task = Task.new(frames, 1, "", false, ->(task) { false }, action)
    add_task(task)
    return task
  end

  # =================================================================+
  # Schedules an action to execute repeatedly until a condition is met.
  #
  # Parameters:
  # => frames: The number of frames between executions. 60fps.
  # => action: A lambda block containing the action that will occur.
  # => end_condition: A lambda block (given the task) which returns
  #                   true when you want the task to stop.
  # => name: Optional parameter for naming the task so you can cancel
  #          the task from other areas of the game.
  # =================================================================+
  def every(frames, action, end_condition, name = "")
    task = Task.new(frames, 999999, name, false, end_condition, action)
    return add_task(task)
  end

  # =================================================================+
  # Schedules an action to occur a certain number of times with the
  # given number of frames between each execution. The first execution
  # is 1 frame after scheduling.
  #
  # Parameters:
  # => times: The number of times it will execute.
  # => frames: The number of frames between each time. 60fps.
  # => action: A lambda block containing the action that will occur.
  # =================================================================+
  def do_times(times, frames, action)
    # The end condition is always false, and it starts immediately.
    task = Task.new(frames, times, "", true, ->(task) { false }, action)
    return add_task(task)
  end

  # Adds a task to the scheduler. This uses a Task object (see above)
  def add_task(task)
    @tasks << task
    return task
  end

  # Removes tasks from the scheduler where they have the given name.
  def remove(name)
    @tasks.delete_if { |task| task.name == name }
  end

  # Runs the update on all the tasks. You probably don't need to change this.
  def update
    @tasks.delete_if { |task| task.should_continue == false }
  end
end
============= SCRIPT 137: Sylph - TimedTarget =============
#==============================================================================
# ** TimedTarget
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Version:  1.0.1
# Date:     2/08/2020
# Author:   SylphPhoenix
#
# This is a generalized class for a quick time event, usually based on a target
# and rapidly closing ring. While that is what occurs with all default options,
# with a small number of input parameters the ring can be removed, as well as
# the small window for input, the required inputs, the icons themselves, and a
# lot more.
#
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The process of using a timed target is simple. Observe the below example.
#
# options = {
#   :quadrant => :random_fourways,
#   :duration => 180,
#   :bloom => 0.6
# }
# target = TimedTarget.new(options)
# target.start
# wait_until -> { target.active == false }
# if (target.result == :success)
#   run_something
# else
#   run_something_else
# end
#
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The potential options, as well as their purpose and default, are below.
#
# :target_image
#    The target's image path. Defaults to "Crow/PlatebodyOff/Hud/Target.png"
#
# :target_color
#    The color to be applied to the target, as a Color object. Default is nil.
#
# :ring_image
#    The ring's image path. Defaults to "Crow/PlatebodyOff/Hud/Ring.png"
#
# :ring_color
#    The color to be applied to the ring, as a Color object. Default is nil.
#
# :hide_target
#    Whether the target sprite should be hidden. Defaults to false.
#
# :hide_ring
#    Whether the ring sprite should be hidden. Defaults to false.
#
# :duration
#    The duration of the quick time event in frames. Defaults to 120 (2s)
#
# :persist_duration
#    The amount of time, in frames, that the sprites will remain on screen
#    after success/failure before it marks the QTE as inactive. This is just a
#    grace period to make the effect less jarring. Defaults to 10.
#
# :on_success
#    The action to take on success, before any pausing. Defaults to empty.
#
# :on_fail
#    The action to take on failure, before any pausing. Defaults to empty.
#
# :anytime
#    Whether it is ok to complete the QTE before the ring has closed in enough.
#    Defaults to false.
#
# :harsh
#    Whether inputting the correct input outside of the right time causes the
#    QTE to fail. Defaults to true.
#
# :bloom
#    The scale applied to the ring sprite to use as the outer boundary. Making
#    this small means the ring would move slowly and from a smaller start
#    size, giving more time in the correct range. Large is the opposite.
#    Defaults to 1 because the default ring is pretty big.
#
# :scale
#    The scale applied to both the target and ring sprites. This is used to
#    "scale up" the entire QTE.
#
# :rotation
#    The angle (in degrees) that the target should be rotated. 90 is left,
#    -90 or 270 is right, etc. Defaults to 0.
#
# :quadrant
#    The section of the screen the target will be placed. The possible values
#    include :random, :random_fourway, :top_left, :top_middle, :top_right,
#    :right_middle, :bottom_right, :bottom_middle, :bottom_left, :middle_left,
#    :center.
#
#    :random will give a random other quadrant, and :random_fourways will give
#    a random but only including :left_middle, :right_middle, :top_middle, and
#    :bottom_middle.
#
#    The quadrant will also automatically set an appropriate value for
#    :input_pass and :attempting_input. These values can be overwritten by
#    specifying them in the options explicitly.
#
# :x
#    The x coordinate of the sprites. Not necessary if you provide a quadrant.
#    Defaults to 272 (screen center)
#
# :y
#    The y coordinate of the sprites. Not necessary if you provide a quadrant.
#    Defaults to 208 (screen center)
#
# :input_pass
#    A lambda that returns true when the player is inputting the correct keys
#    for this target. This defaults to true, or to an appropriate value if the
#    quadrant has been set.
#
# :attempting_input
#    A lambda that returns true when the player is inputting keys that suggest
#    that they are probably trying. The result of this lambda is used only for
#    the failing of the QTE by bad input, so this option has no meaning when
#    :anytime is true and :harsh is false. This defaults to false, or to an
#    appropriate value if the quadrant has been set.
#
#==============================================================================

class TimedTarget

  attr_accessor :pos_x, :pos_y, :inert, :status
  #=======================================================================
  # Use this section for creating shortcut initializers.
  #-----------------------------------------------------------------------

  # Convenience for making a left-facing target with left keyboard input
  def self.left(options = nil)
    options = {} if options.nil?
    options[:rotation] = 90
    options[:input_pass] = -> { return Input.left_only? }
    options[:attempting_input] = -> { return Input.any_direction? }
    return TimedTarget.new(options)
  end


  #========================================================================

  def initialize(options = nil)
    if options.nil?
      options = Hash.new
    end
    
    #Koda 
    @inert = options.fetch(:inert, false)

    @target_image = options.fetch(:target_image, "Hud/Target/Target.png")
    @target_color = options.fetch(:target_color, nil)
    @ring_image = options.fetch(:ring_image, "Hud/Target/Ring.png")
    @ring_color = options.fetch(:ring_color, nil)

    @rotation = options.fetch(:rotation, 0);

    @anytime = options.fetch(:anytime, false)

    @duration = options.fetch(:duration, 120)
    
    #Koda - Rhythm
    @duration += (You.rhythm * 10)
    
    @scale = options.fetch(:scale, 1.2).to_f

    @sprites_loaded = false

    @on_success = options[:on_success] unless !options.has_key?(:on_success)
    @on_fail = options[:on_fail] unless !options.has_key?(:on_fail)

    @hide_ring = options.fetch(:hide_ring, false)
    @hide_target = options.fetch(:hide_target, false)

    @harsh = options.fetch(:harsh, true)
    @pos_x = options.fetch(:x, 272)
    @pos_y = options.fetch(:y, 208)
    @bloom = options.fetch(:bloom, 1).to_f
    @persist_duration = options.fetch(:persist_duration, 5)

    @pos_z = 99

    quadrant = options[:quadrant]
    if quadrant == :random
      quadrant = [:top_left, :top_middle, :top_right, :middle_right, :bottom_right, :bottom_middle, :bottom_left, :middle_left, :center].sample
    elsif quadrant == :random_fourway
      quadrant = [:middle_left, :middle_right, :top_middle, :bottom_middle].sample
    end

    if quadrant.nil? == false && quadrant != :center
      @pos_y = 416 / 4 if [:top_left, :top_middle, :top_right].include?(quadrant)
      @pos_y = 3 * 416 / 4 if [:bottom_left, :bottom_middle, :bottom_right].include?(quadrant)

      @pos_x = 544 / 4 if [:top_left, :middle_left, :bottom_left].include?(quadrant)
      @pos_x = 3 * 544 / 4 if [:top_right, :middle_right, :bottom_right].include?(quadrant)

      complex_attempt = -> { return (Input.any_direction? && Input.press?(:C)) || Mouse.left_click? }
      simple_attempt = -> { return Mouse.left_click? || Input.any_direction? || Input.press?(:C) }

      case quadrant
        when :top_left
          @input_pass = -> { return (Input.all_of?(:UP, :LEFT) && Input.none_of?(:DOWN, :RIGHT)) || (Mouse.left_click? && Mouse.top_leftish?) }
          @attempting_input = complex_attempt
        when :middle_left
          @input_pass = -> { return Input.left_only? || (Mouse.left_click? && Mouse.leftish?) }
          @attempting_input = simple_attempt
        when :bottom_left
          @input_pass = -> { return (Input.all_of?(:DOWN, :LEFT) && Input.none_of?(:UP, :RIGHT)) || (Mouse.left_click? && Mouse.bottom_leftish?) }
          @attempting_input = complex_attempt
        when :bottom_middle
          @input_pass = -> { return Input.down_only? || (Mouse.left_click? && Mouse.downish?)}
          @attempting_input = simple_attempt
        when :bottom_right
          @input_pass = -> { return (Input.all_of?(:DOWN, :RIGHT) && Input.none_of?(:UP, :LEFT)) || (Mouse.left_click? && Mouse.bottom_rightish?) }
          @attempting_input = complex_attempt
        when :middle_right
          @input_pass = -> { return Input.right_only? || (Mouse.left_click? && Mouse.rightish?)}
          @attempting_input = simple_attempt
        when :top_right
          @input_pass = -> { return (Input.all_of?(:UP, :RIGHT) && !Input.none_of?(:DOWN, :LEFT)) || (Mouse.left_click? && Mouse.top_rightish?) }
          @attempting_input = complex_attempt
        when :top_middle
          @input_pass = -> { return Input.up_only? || (Mouse.left_click? && Mouse.upish?)}
          @attempting_input = simple_attempt
      end
    else
      # Center
      typical = -> { return Input.trigger?(:C) || Mouse.left_click? }
      @attempting_input = typical unless options.has_key?(:attempting_input)
      @input_pass = typical unless options.has_key?(:input_pass)
    end

    @input_pass = options[:input_pass] unless !options.has_key?(:input_pass)
    @attempting_input = options[:attempting_input] unless !options.has_key?(:attempting_input)

    @remaining = @duration
  end

  # Instantiates the sprites to use for the icons. At the same time, it
  # calculates what percentage of time must be elapsed for the ring to just be
  # closing around the target based on bitmap sizes.
  def load_sprites
    @target_sprite = Sprite.new
    @ring_sprite = Sprite.new

    @target_sprite.bitmap = Cache.picture(@target_image)
    @target_sprite.color = @target_color unless @target_color.nil?
    @target_sprite.opacity = 0

    @ring_sprite.bitmap = Cache.picture(@ring_image)
    @ring_sprite.color = @ring_color unless @ring_color.nil?
    @ring_sprite.opacity = 0

    # The 0.86 is because of the thickness of the default ring. Might need another
    # option to prevent this bit of hardcoding.
    @acceptance_percentage = @target_sprite.bitmap.width / (@ring_sprite.bitmap.width * @bloom * 0.86)

    @target_sprite.x = @ring_sprite.x = @pos_x
    @target_sprite.y = @ring_sprite.y = @pos_y
    @target_sprite.z = @ring_sprite.z = @pos_z

    @target_sprite.ox = @target_sprite.bitmap.width / 2
    @target_sprite.oy = @target_sprite.bitmap.height / 2

    @ring_sprite.ox = @ring_sprite.bitmap.width / 2
    @ring_sprite.oy = @ring_sprite.bitmap.height / 2

    @target_sprite.zoom_x = @target_sprite.zoom_y = @scale
    @target_sprite.angle = @rotation

    @sprites_loaded = true
  end

  def set_on_fail(action)
    @on_fail = action
  end

  def set_on_success(action)
    @on_success = action
  end

  def active
    @active
  end
  
  #Koda
  def enable
    @inert = false
  end 

  def failed
    return @status == :fail_by_timeout || @status == :fail_by_input
  end

  def succeeded
    return @status == :success
  end

  def start
    load_sprites unless @sprites_loaded

    @active = true
    @remaining = @duration
    @target_sprite.opacity = 255 unless @hide_target
    @ring_sprite.zoom_x = @ring_sprite.zoom_y = @bloom * @scale
    @ring_sprite.opacity = 255 unless @hide_ring

    # Keep running update until @active is false
    $scheduler.every(1, -> { update }, -> { @active == false })
  end

  def stop
    @target_sprite.dispose
    @ring_sprite.dispose
    @active = false
    @hold = false
  end

  def result
    return @status
  end

  def on_qte_finished
    if @persist_duration == 0
      stop
    else
      @hold = true
      $scheduler.after(@persist_duration, -> { stop })
    end
  end

  def update
    if @hold
      @target_sprite.update
      @ring_sprite.update
      return
    end
    @remaining = @remaining - 1

    completed_percentage = @remaining.to_f / @duration.to_f
    zoom = completed_percentage * @bloom * @scale
    @ring_sprite.zoom_x = @ring_sprite.zoom_y = zoom

    if @remaining <= 0
      @status = :fail_by_timeout
      @on_fail.call unless @on_fail.nil?
      on_qte_finished
      return
    end

    #p "inert is: " + @inert.to_s
    # This first check is just to make sure it doesn't catch input from before, like clicking through dialogue
    if @inert == false #Koda
      if completed_percentage < 0.9
        # If we're in the acceptance timeframe and they're inputting the correct keys
        if (completed_percentage <= @acceptance_percentage || @anytime) && @input_pass.call == true
          @status = :success
          @on_success.call unless @on_success.nil?
          on_qte_finished
          return
        # They are attempting input. If @harsh, being correct but being out of the time frame is a fail. If bad input, also fail.
        elsif @attempting_input.call == true && (@input_pass.call == false || @harsh)
          @status = :fail_by_input
          @on_fail.call unless @on_fail.nil?
          on_qte_finished
          return
        end
      end
    end

    @target_sprite.update
    @ring_sprite.update
  end
end

# Some useful additions to Mouse.
module Mouse
  class << self
    def leftish?
      return Mouse.pos[0] < 272
    end

    def rightish?
      return Mouse.pos[0] > 272
    end

    def downish?
      return Mouse.pos[1] > 208 # In games, smaller Y is higher on the screen
    end

    def upish?
      return Mouse.pos[1] < 208 # In games, smaller Y is higher on the screen
    end

    def top_leftish?
      return upish? && leftish?
    end

    def top_rightish?
      return upish? && rightish?
    end

    def bottom_leftish?
      return downish? && leftish?
    end

    def bottom_rightish?
      return downish? && rightish?
    end

    def left_click?
      return Mouse.trigger?(0)
    end

    def right_click?
      return Mouse.trigger?(2)
    end

    def middle_click?
      return Mouse.trigger?(1)
    end
  end
end

# Some useful additions to Input
module Input
  class << self
    def left_not_right?
      return Input.press?(:LEFT) && !Input.press?(:RIGHT)
    end

    def right_not_left?
      return Input.press?(:RIGHT) && !Input.press?(:LEFT)
    end

    def up_not_down?
      return Input.press?(:UP) && !Input.press?(:DOWN)
    end

    def down_not_up?
      return Input.press?(:DOWN) && !Input.press?(:UP)
    end

    def left_only?
      return left_not_right? && no_vertical?
    end

    def right_only?
      return right_not_left? && no_vertical?
    end

    def up_only?
      return up_not_down? && no_horizontal?
    end

    def down_only?
      return down_not_up? && no_horizontal?
    end

    def no_vertical?
      return !Input.press?(:UP) && !Input.press?(:DOWN)
    end

    def any_vertical?
      return Input.press?(:UP) || Input.press?(:DOWN)
    end

    def no_horizontal?
      return !Input.press?(:LEFT) && !Input.press?(:RIGHT)
    end

    def any_horizontal?
      return Input.press?(:LEFT) || Input.press?(:RIGHT)
    end

    def no_direction?
      return no_vertical? && no_horizontal?
    end

    def any_direction?
      return any_vertical? || any_horizontal?
    end

    def no_input?
      return no_vertical? && no_horizontal? && !Input.press?(:C)
    end

    def any_input?
      return any_vertical? || any_horizontal? || Input.press?(:C)
    end

    def all_of?(*directions)
      directions.each {|dir| return false if !Input.press?(dir)}
      return true
    end

    def none_of?(*directions)
      directions.each {|dir| return false if Input.press?(dir)}
      return true
    end
  end
end
============= SCRIPT 138: Sylph - PingPong =============
#==============================================================================
# ** PingPong
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Version:  1.0.0
# Date:     15/08/2021
# Author:   SylphPhoenix
#
# This is a generalized class for a quick time event, based around the user
# needing to input at a particular moment as a cursor moves between two ends
# of a bar. Options exist for asset control, cursor speed, sizing, and the
# handling of hits and misses on input.
#
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The process of using a ping pong is simple but it's up to your use case to
# decide when it should stop and the ping_pong.stop function should run.
#
# successes = 0
# options = {
#   :on_hit => ->(ping_pong) {
#     p "Hit!"
#     successes = successes + 1
#     if successes >= 5
#        ping_pong.stop
#     else
#        ping_pong.choose_hitzone
#     end
#   },
#   :on_miss => ->(ping_pong) { p "Miss!" }
# }
# ping_pong = PingPong.new(options)
# ping_pong.start
# wait_until -> { ping_pong.active == false }
#
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The potential options, as well as their purpose and default, are below.
#
# :cursor
#    The asset for the cursor. Defaults to "Swan/Hud/PingPong/Cursor1.png"
#
# :base
#    The asset for the base of the bar. This is meant to be a hollow outline for
#    the bar. Defaults to "Swan/Hud/PingPong/Meter2.png"
#
# :bar_colour
#    The colour to use for the majority of the bar (not including the hitzone).
#    Defaults to Color.new(36, 67, 68, 204) which was used in Swan.
#
# :hitzone_colour
#    The colour to use for the hitzone of the bar. Defaults to what was used for
#    swan, Color.new(70, 0, 21, 255).
#
# :pos_x
#    The x position of the ping pong. Defaults to 80.
#
# :pos_y
#    The y position of the ping pong. Defaults to 140.
#
# :bar_offset_left
#    The left offset for drawing the bar inside the base asset. This is so that
#    the bar will be drawn precisely inside the borders of the base asset.
#    Defaults to 2, which is the right value for Meter2.
#
# :bar_offset_right
#    The right offset for drawing the bar inside the base asset. This is so that
#    the bar will be drawn precisely inside the borders of the base asset.
#    Defaults to 2, which is the right value for Meter2.
#
# :bar_offset_up
#    The up offset for drawing the bar inside the base asset. This is so that
#    the bar will be drawn precisely inside the borders of the base asset.
#    Defaults to 2, which is the right value for Meter2.
#
# :bar_offset_down
#    The down offset for drawing the bar inside the base asset. This is so that
#    the bar will be drawn precisely inside the borders of the base asset.
#    Defaults to 2, which is the right value for Meter2.
#
# :cursor_offset_vertical
#    The vertical offset for drawing the cursor against the bar. This is so that
#    the cursor lines up where you want it to on the bar. Defaults to 20 which
#    is not a bad value for the default cursor and base assets.
#
# :hitzone_width
#    The width of the hitzone in pixels. Making this smaller makes it more
#    difficult to hit, particularly when the cursor speed is high. Defaults
#    to 40 but can be changed here and during operation.
#
# :hitzone_x
#    The x coordinate of the hitzone. This is the left end of the hitzone. It is
#    not including the left offset, so this value should move between 0 and the
#    base asset's width minus the left and right offsets. Defaults to a derived
#    value which puts it exactly in the middle of the bar.
#
# :on_hit
#    The lambda function to run when the player successfully lands a hit in the
#    hitzone. There is one parameter to this function, which is the ping pong
#    object itself. This can be used to play a sound, or move the hitzone, or
#    whatever you like.
#
# :on_miss
#    The lambda function to run when the player tries htiting the hitzone but
#    misses. There is one parameter to this function, which is the ping pong
#    object itself. This can be used to play a sound, or move the hitzone, or
#    whatever you like.
#
# :on_direction_swap
#    The lambda function to run when the cursor reaches one end of the bar and
#    is about to move in the other direction. There is one parameter to this
#    function, which is the ping pong object itself.
#
# :inputting
#    The lambda function that returns true when the player is pressing the hit
#    button. Defaults to one such that pressing Z or left clicking returns true.
#
# :max_lifetime
#    The maximum number of frames the ping pong will remain active. If this is
#    not left at -1 (which it is by default) then once it reaches this number
#    of frames on-screen it will automatically stop.
#
#=============================================================================+
class PingPong
  attr_accessor :active, :cursor_position, :cursor_speed, :hitzone_x, :hitzone_width

  def initialize(options)
    @cursor = options.fetch(:cursor, "Swan/Hud/PingPong/Cursor1.png")
    @base = options.fetch(:base, "Swan/Hud/PingPong/Meter2.png")
    @bar_colour = options.fetch(:bar_colour, Color.new(36, 67, 68, 204))
    @hitzone_colour = options.fetch(:hitzone_colour, Color.new(70, 0, 21, 255))
    @pos_x = options.fetch(:pos_x, 80)
    @pos_y = options.fetch(:pos_y, 140)
    @bar_offset_left = options.fetch(:bar_offset_left, 2)
    @bar_offset_right = options.fetch(:bar_offset_right, 2)
    @bar_offset_up = options.fetch(:bar_offset_up, 2)
    @bar_offset_down = options.fetch(:bar_offset_down, 2)
    @cursor_offset_vertical = options.fetch(:cursor_offset_vertical, 20)
    @hitzone_width = options.fetch(:hitzone_width, 40)
    @on_hit = options.fetch(:on_hit, ->(ping_pong) { })
    @on_miss = options.fetch(:on_miss, ->(ping_pong) { })
    @on_direction_swap = options.fetch(:on_direction_swap, ->(ping_pong) { })
    @inputting = options.fetch(:inputting, ->{ return Input.trigger?(:C) || Mouse.left_click? })
    @max_lifetime = options.fetch(:max_lifetime, -1)
    
    @cursor_speed = options.fetch(:cursor_speed, 5)
    
    @cursor_position = 0
    @cursor_sprite = Sprite.new
    @cursor_sprite.bitmap = Cache.picture(@cursor)
    @cursor_sprite.x = @cursor_position + @pos_x + @bar_offset_left - @cursor_sprite.bitmap.width / 2
    @cursor_sprite.y = @pos_y + @cursor_offset_vertical
    @cursor_sprite.z = 1003#3
    @bar_sprite = Sprite.new
    @bar_sprite.x = @pos_x + @bar_offset_left
    @bar_sprite.y = @pos_y + @bar_offset_up
    @bar_sprite.z = 1001#1
    @base_sprite = Sprite.new
    @base_sprite.bitmap = Cache.picture(@base)
    @base_sprite.x = @pos_x
    @base_sprite.y = @pos_y
    @base_sprite.z = 1002#2

    @bar_width = @base_sprite.bitmap.width - @bar_offset_left - @bar_offset_right
    @bar_height = @base_sprite.bitmap.height - @bar_offset_up - @bar_offset_down

    @hitzone_x = options.fetch(:hitzone_x, @bar_width / 2 - @hitzone_width / 2)

    update_bar_drawing
  end

  def update_bar_drawing
    bitmap = Bitmap.new(@bar_width, @bar_height)
    bitmap.fill_rect(0, 0, @bar_width, @bar_height, @bar_colour)
    bitmap.fill_rect(@hitzone_x, 0, @hitzone_width, @bar_height, @hitzone_colour)
    @bar_sprite.bitmap = bitmap
  end

  def choose_hitzone
    @hitzone_x = rand(@bar_width - @hitzone_width)
    update_bar_drawing
  end

  def start
    @active = true
    @direction = :right
    @lifetime = 0

    $scheduler.every(1, ->{ update }, ->{ return @active == false })
  end

  def update
    clicking = @inputting.call
    if clicking
      if @cursor_position >= @hitzone_x && @cursor_position <= @hitzone_x + @hitzone_width
        # nice
        @on_hit.call(self)
      else
        # less nice
        @on_miss.call(self)
      end
    end

    return if @active == false

    @cursor_position = @cursor_position + @cursor_speed if @direction == :right
    @cursor_position = @cursor_position - @cursor_speed if @direction == :left

    if @cursor_position <= 0
      @cursor_position = @cursor_position * -1
      @direction = :right
      @on_direction_swap.call(self)
    elsif @cursor_position >= @bar_width
      @cursor_position = @bar_width - (@cursor_position - @bar_width) # Could be simplified to 2B - C but this way is more clear
      @direction = :left
      @on_direction_swap.call(self)
    end

    @lifetime = @lifetime + 1
    if @lifetime > @max_lifetime && @max_lifetime > 0
      stop
      return
    end

    @cursor_sprite.x = @cursor_position + @pos_x + @bar_offset_left - @cursor_sprite.bitmap.width / 2

    @bar_sprite.update
    @base_sprite.update
    @cursor_sprite.update
  end

  def stop
    @active = false
    @cursor_sprite.dispose
    @base_sprite.dispose
    @bar_sprite.dispose
  end
end
============= SCRIPT 139: Sylph - Button Mash =============
#==============================================================================
# ** ButtonMash
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Version:  1.0.1
# Date:     10/08/2021
# Author:   SylphPhoenix
#
# This is a generalized class for a quick time event, based around the user
# needing to input a single or sequence of buttons in rapid succession. Options
# exist for enforcing a minimum and maximum time between inputs, maximum time
# before first input, the requirements on input, the necessary correct inputs,
# and an output stream for progress.
#
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The process of using a button mash is simple but needs to be connected to UI
# elements yourself. Observe the below example for everything except UI.
#
# options = {
#   :max_lifetime = 120,
#   :input_times = 10,
#   :on_progress = ->(button_mash, progress){ p 'They are at ' + progress.to_s }
# }
# mash = ButtonMash.new(options)
# mash.start
# wait_until -> { mash.active == false }
# if (mash.succeeded)
#   run_something
# else
#   run_something_else
# end
#
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The potential options, as well as their purpose and default, are below.
#
# :max_lifetime
#    The number of frames they are given to mash. Defaults to -1 which means
#    there is no timeline.
#
# :max_time_before_begin
#    The number of frames given as a grace period before they need to provide
#    inputs. This is for situations where you are using the constraints on the
#    time between inputs. Dfaults to -1 which means there is an infinite grace
#    period.
#
# :min_time_between_inputs
#    The minimum number of frames that must be left between inputs, or else the
#    quick time event will fail. This is for when you want a steady tempo from
#    the player rather than just speed. Defaults to -1, meaning there is no
#    minimum.
#
# :max_time_between_inputs
#    The maximum number of frames that must be left between inputs, or else the
#    quick time event will fail. This is for when you want the player to hurry
#    the fuck up. Defaults to -1, meaning there is no maximum.
#
# :ignore_input
#    Whether or not the button mash will be input agnostic. This setting, when
#    true, will set the input_series and input_attempted fields to be such that
#    no inputs will count as good or bad inputs.
#
# :input_series
#    This is an array of lambda functions that return true when the player is
#    giving valid input. The idea is that you may wish for the player to need
#    to mash left and right over and over. In this case, it is an array of two
#    lambdas, one that returns true if left is pressed and one for right. For a
#    single input button mash, just make this a single-element array. This
#    defaults to a single-element array where pressing Z or clicking is input.
#
# :input_attempted
#    This is a lambda that returns true if the player is inputting something.
#    This is used to detect when the player is incorrectly inputting.
#
# :input_times
#    The number of times the player must correctly input the series. If this is
#    set to something that isn't -1, the button mash will 'succeed' once their
#    progress reaches this number.
#
# :on_progress
#    The progress output stream of the button mash. This is a lambda function
#    with two input variables, button_mash and progress. The button_mash input
#    is this instance of the button mash (for ease), while the progress is the
#    number of correct inputs in series.
#
# :on_good_input
#    A lambda function to run when the player inputs the correct next button
#    in the series. The lambda function has one input variable, button_mash,
#    which is a reference to the button mash itself. The default value of this
#    option increases the progress of the button mash by 1.
#
# :on_bad_input
#    A lambda function to run when the player inputs something but it is not
#    the correct input for the current part of the input series. The lambda
#    function has one input variable, button_mash, which is a reference to the
#    button mash itself. The default value of this option is such that every bad
#    input removes 1 point from the progress of the mash.
#
# :on_failure
#    A lambda function that runs when the player has failed the button mash
#    and the quick time event is ending. This occurs if they took too long for
#    their first input, too much or too little time between inputs, running
#    out of time, or something you've done yourself with another option. There
#    is an input to the lambda, button_mash, which is a reference to the button
#    mash itself.
#
# :on_success
#    A lambda function that runs when the player has succeeded at the button
#    mash. Success could mean that they have supplied the desired number of
#    inputs in series (if input_times is set) or that they survived the button
#    mash long enough (if input_times is blank but max_lifetime is not).
#
# :succeed_at_max_progress
#    A true or false value representing what happens when the player reaches max
#    progress (as in getting progress to equal to input_times). When true, this
#    will complete the button mash. When false, it will still be at max progress
#    but will otherwise do nothing and the mash will continue.
#
#==============================================================================
class ButtonMash
  def initialize(options)
    @max_lifetime = options.fetch(:max_lifetime, -1)
    @max_time_before_begin = options.fetch(:max_time_before_begin, -1)
    @min_time_between_inputs = options.fetch(:min_time_between_inputs, -1)
    @max_time_between_inputs = options.fetch(:max_time_between_inputs, -1)

    ignore_input = options.fetch(:ignore_input, false)
    if ignore_input
      @input_series = [ -> { return false } ]
      @input_attempted = -> { return false }
    end

    @input_series = options.fetch(:input_series, [-> { return Input.press?(:C) || Mouse.left_click? }])
    @input_attempted = options.fetch(:input_attempted, -> { return @input_series.any? { |input| return input.call == true } })
    @input_times = options.fetch(:input_times, -1)

    @on_progress = options.fetch(:on_progress, ->(button_mash, progress) { })
    @on_good_input = options.fetch(:on_good_input, ->(button_mash) { button_mash.increase_progress })
    @on_bad_input = options.fetch(:on_bad_input, ->(button_mash) { button_mash.decrease_progress })
    @on_failure = options.fetch(:on_failure, ->(button_mash) { })
    @on_success = options.fetch(:on_success, -> { })

    @succeed_at_max_progress = options.fetch(:succeed_at_max_progress, true)

    @input_length = @input_series.length
  end

  def start
    @active = true
    @last_input = 0
    @lifetime = 0
    @input_index = 0
    @progress = 0

    # Keep running update until @active is false
    $scheduler.every(1, -> { update }, -> { @active == false })
  end

  def stop
    @active = false
  end

  def update
    @lifetime = @lifetime + 1

    will_progress = @input_series[@input_index].call == true
    will_fail = will_progress == false && @input_attempted.call

    time_since_input = @lifetime - @last_input
    if will_progress == false && will_fail == false
      if @progress == 0 && time_since_input > @max_time_before_begin && @max_time_before_begin > 0
        fail
        return
      elsif @progress > 0 && time_since_input > @max_time_between_inputs && @max_time_between_inputs > 0
        fail
        return
      end
    end

    if will_progress
      if time_since_input < @min_time_between_inputs && @min_time_between_inputs > 0
        fail
        return
      end
      @on_good_input.call(self)
      @input_index = @input_index + 1
      if @input_index >= @input_length
        @input_index = 0
      end
      @last_input = @lifetime
    end

    if @active && @lifetime > @max_lifetime && @max_lifetime > 0
      if @input_times == -1
        succeed
      else
        fail
      end
      return
    end

    if will_fail
      @on_bad_input.call(self)
    end
  end

  def progress
    return @progress
  end

  def increase_progress(amount = 1)
    previous_progress = @progress
    @progress = @progress + amount
    if @progress > @input_times
      @progress = @input_times
    end
    @on_progress.call(self, @progress) if previous_progress != @progress
    if @progress == @input_times && @succeed_at_max_progress == true
      succeed
    end
  end

  def decrease_progress(amount = 1)
    previous_progress = @progress
    @progress = @progress - amount
    if @progress < 0
      @progress = 0
    end
    @on_progress.call(self, @progress) if previous_progress != @progress
  end

  def set_progress(progress)
    previous_progress = @progress
    @progress = progress
    @on_progress.call(self, @progress) if previous_progress != @progress
  end

  def succeed
    @result = :succeeded
    @on_success.call
    stop
  end

  def fail
    @result = :failed
    @on_failure.call(self)
    stop
  end

  def input_index
    return @input_index
  end

  def active
    return @active
  end

  def result
    return @result
  end

  def succeeded
    return result == :succeeded
  end

  def failed
    return result == :failed
  end
end
============= SCRIPT 140: Sylph - Progressable Bar =============
#==============================================================================
# ** ProgressableBar
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Version:  1.0.0
# Date:     14/08/2021
# Author:   SylphPhoenix
#
# Generalized progress bar UI script. It revolves around a progress and a max
# progress, with a bar based on the progress between those two points. The
# progress of the bar is updated using the
# update_progress(progress, max_progress) function. This is designed to be used
# alongside the ButtonMash component, as a simple progress output stream UI.
#
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The potential options, as well as their purpose and default, are below.
#
# :base_bar
#    The sprite to use as the bar frame's image. This should be made such that
#    the progress bar can be drawn under it, so the inside of the bar should be
#    transparent.
#
# :base_colour
#    The colour function to apply to the base bar. This is a lambda function
#    with a single parameter, the percentage of progress. This can return a
#    different colour based on the current progress. This is applied as a sprite
#    hue. This can and probably should return nil.
#
# :fill_bar
#    The sprite to use as the bar's filled in portion. This might be a plain
#    white pixel asset so that it can be reshaped and recoloured. It will be
#    drawn underneath the base bar.
#
# # :fill_colour
#    The colour function to apply to the bar's filling. This is a lambda
#    function with a single parameter, the percentage of progress. This can
#    return a different colour based on the current progress. This is applied as
#    a sprite hue. If it returns nil, then the fill bar will have no hue
#    applied to it. This is especially useful if the fill_bar is a white asset.
#
# :x
#    The x coordinate to place the sprites. Defaults to screen centre.
#
# :y
#    The y coordinate to place the sprites. Defaults to screen centre.
#
# :initial_percentage
#    The initial percentage of the fill bar. This is used in lieu of immediately
#    running the update_progress function of the bar to set it prior to draw.
#
# :rotation
#    The rotation (degrees) to apply to both the frame and the filling. 90 is a
#    left rotation I think, I don't really remember.
#
#==============================================================================
class ProgressableBar
  attr_accessor :active

  def initialize(options)
    @base_bar = options.fetch(:base_bar, "Hud/Bars/Base_Bar.png")
    @base_colour = options.fetch(:base_colour, ->(percentage) { return nil })
    #@fill_bar = options.fetch(:fill_bar, "Hud/Bars/White_Fill.png")
    @fill_colour = options.fetch(:fill_colour, ->(percentage) { return [Color.new(0, 0, 0, 255), Color.new(255, 0, 0, 255)] })
    @pos_x = options.fetch(:x, 272)
    @pos_y = options.fetch(:y, 208)
    @rotation = options.fetch(:rotation, 0)
    @left_offset = options.fetch(:left_offset, 2)
    @right_offset = options.fetch(:right_offset, 2)
    @up_offset = options.fetch(:up_offset, 0)
    @down_offset = options.fetch(:down_offset, 0)

    @base_bar_sprite = Sprite.new
    @fill_bar_sprite = Sprite.new

    @base_bar_sprite.bitmap = Cache.picture(@base_bar)
    @base_bar_sprite.x = @pos_x
    @base_bar_sprite.y = @pos_y
    @base_bar_sprite.angle = @rotation

    @fill_bar_sprite.x = @pos_x
    @fill_bar_sprite.y = @pos_y
    @fill_bar_sprite.angle = @rotation

    initial_percentage = options.fetch(:initial_percentage, 0)
    @active = true
    # @fill_bar_sprite.bitmap.width = (initial_percentage / 100) * @max_width

    @base_bar_sprite.z = 1005#2
    @fill_bar_sprite.z = 1004#1

    @base_bar_sprite.color = @base_colour.call(initial_percentage) unless @base_colour.call(initial_percentage).nil?

    adjust_fill(initial_percentage)

    $scheduler.every(1, -> { update }, -> { @active == false })
  end

  def base_bar_sprite
    return @base_bar_sprite
  end

  def fill_bar_sprite
    return @fill_bar_sprite
  end

  def update
    @fill_bar_sprite.update
    @base_bar_sprite.update
  end

  def stop
    @base_bar_sprite.dispose
    @fill_bar_sprite.dispose
    @active = false
  end

  def adjust_fill(percentage)
    gradient_bitmap = Bitmap.new(@base_bar_sprite.bitmap.width, @base_bar_sprite.bitmap.height)
    colours = @fill_colour.call(percentage)
    gradient_bitmap.gradient_fill_rect(0, 0, @base_bar_sprite.bitmap.width - @left_offset - @right_offset, @base_bar_sprite.bitmap.height, colours[0], colours[1])
    @fill_bar_sprite.bitmap = Bitmap.new(gradient_bitmap.width, gradient_bitmap.height)
    x = @left_offset
    max_x = @base_bar_sprite.bitmap.width - @right_offset
    src_rect = Rect.new(0, 0, gradient_bitmap.width * percentage / 100, gradient_bitmap.height)
    @fill_bar_sprite.bitmap.blt(0, 0, gradient_bitmap, src_rect)
  end

  def update_progress(progress, max_progress)
    percentage = (progress.to_f / max_progress) * 100
    base_colour = @base_colour.call(percentage)
    @base_bar_sprite.color = base_colour unless base_colour.nil?
    adjust_fill(percentage)
  end
end
============= SCRIPT 141:  =============

============= SCRIPT 142: Sylph - Item Tooltips  =============
=begin
#==============================================================================
# ** Item Tooltips
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Version:  1.0.0
# Date:     28/09/2021
# Author:   SylphPhoenix
#
# This injects additional functionality into the help window and item selection
# windows. Pressing the W key (:R) when an item selection pane is open will
# change the help window to display tooltip text instead. The text to display
# for each item can be set below, and failing that it will show blank text.
#
# If there is another window that uses the help window but is not
# Window_ItemList, copy paste the bottom class injection and change it slightly
# for the window you want this functionality merged into.
#
#==============================================================================

# Maps item ID to tooltip text.
I_TOOLTIPS = []
W_TOOLTIPS = []
A_TOOLTIPS = []

I_TOOLTIPS[101] = "Shitty fucking herb, more like a weed dude."
W_TOOLTIPS[111] = "Windy sword wwwww"
A_TOOLTIPS[117] = "Insulated from all of Khulan's bullshit"

A_TOOLTIPS[139] = "High ATK, but lowers \\i[150]Rhythm"

class Window_Help < Window_Base

  # Inject into initialize to set up defaults.
  alias main_initialize initialize
  def initialize(line_number = 2)
    main_initialize(line_number)
    @show_tooltips = false
    @tooltip = ""
    @item_text = ""
  end

  def toggle_tooltips
    @show_tooltips = !@show_tooltips

    if @show_tooltips
      set_text(@tooltip)
    else
      set_text(@item_text + " [W]")
    end
  end

  # Overwrite set_item to check for whether it should be using a tooltip or not
  def set_item(item)
    if item.nil?
      set_text("")
      @tooltip = ""
      @item_text = ""
    else
      @tooltip = find_tooltip(item)
      @item_text = item.description
      if @show_tooltips
        set_text(@tooltip)
      else
        set_text(item.description)
      end
    end
  end

  def find_tooltip(item)
    tooltip_text = ""
    if item.is_a?(RPG::Item)
      tooltip_text = I_TOOLTIPS[item.id]
    elsif item.is_a?(RPG::Weapon)
      tooltip_text = W_TOOLTIPS[item.id]
    elsif item.is_a?(RPG::Armor)
      tooltip_text = A_TOOLTIPS[item.id]
    end
    if tooltip_text.nil?
      tooltip_text = ""
    end
    return tooltip_text
  end
end

class Window_ItemList < Window_Selectable
  alias regular_update update
  def update
    regular_update

    if Input.trigger?(:R) && self.active
      @help_window.toggle_tooltips
      RPG::SE.new("Cursor2", 80, 100).play
    end
  end
end

class Window_ItemSynthesisList < Window_Selectable
  alias regular_update update
  def update
    regular_update

    if Input.trigger?(:R) && self.active
      @help_window.toggle_tooltips
      RPG::SE.new("Cursor2", 80, 100).play
    end
  end
end
=end

============= SCRIPT 146: KodaYanfy - SupressLog =============
#==============================================================================
# 
# ▼ Koda edit of Yanfly's Anti-Fail Script to Suppress Battle-Log Entirely
# 
#==============================================================================

#==============================================================================
# 
# ▼ Yanfly Engine Ace - Anti-Fail Message v1.00
# -- Last Updated: 2011.12.23
# -- Level: Normal
# -- Requires: n/a
# 
#==============================================================================

$imported = {} if $imported.nil?
$imported["YEA-AntiFailMessage"] = true

#==============================================================================
# ▼ Updates
# =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=
# 2011.12.23 - Started Script and Finished.
# 
#==============================================================================
# ▼ Introduction
# =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=
# For eventers or custom effect makers out there, when using a skill or item on
# an enemy without any damage or status effect changes, a fail message will
# occur even if there are common events or other effects happening. With this
# script, you can insert a notetag into the skill or item noteboxes to prevent
# that fail message from appearing.
# 
#==============================================================================
# ▼ Instructions
# =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=
# To install this script, open up your script editor and copy/paste this script
# to an open slot below ▼ Materials/素材 but above ▼ Main. Remember to save.
# 
#==============================================================================
# ▼ Compatibility
# =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=
# This script is made strictly for RPG Maker VX Ace. It is highly unlikely that
# it will run with RPG Maker VX without adjusting.
# 
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Skill Notetags - These notetags go in the skills notebox in the database.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# <anti fail>
# Insert this notetag inside of a skill's notebox to prevent it from showing a
# failed message despite having custom effects or running a common event.
# 
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Item Notetags - These notetags go in the skills notebox in the database.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# <anti fail>
# Insert this notetag inside of an item's notebox to prevent it from showing a
# failed message despite having custom effects or running a common event.
# 
#==============================================================================
# ▼ Editting anything past this point may potentially result in causing
# computer damage, incontinence, explosion of user's head, coma, death, and/or
# halitosis so edit at your own risk.
#==============================================================================

module YEA
  module REGEXP
  module USABLEITEM
    
    ANTIFAIL = /<(?:SUPRESS_LOG|supress log|suppresslog)>/i
    
  end # USABLEITEM
  end # REGEXP
end # YEA

#==============================================================================
# ■ DataManager
#==============================================================================

module DataManager
  
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # alias method: load_database
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  class <<self; alias load_database_antifail load_database; end
  def self.load_database
    load_database_antifail
    load_notetags_antifail
  end
  
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # new method: load_notetags_antifail
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  def self.load_notetags_antifail
    groups = [$data_skills, $data_items]
    for group in groups
      for obj in group
        next if obj.nil?
        obj.load_notetags_antifail
      end
    end
  end
  
end # DataManager

#==============================================================================
# ■ RPG::UsableItem
#==============================================================================

class RPG::UsableItem < RPG::BaseItem
  
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # public instance variables
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  attr_accessor :antifail
  
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # common cache: load_notetags_antifail
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  def load_notetags_antifail
    @antifail = false
    #---
    self.note.split(/[\r\n]+/).each { |line|
      case line
      #---
      when YEA::REGEXP::USABLEITEM::ANTIFAIL
        @antifail = true
      #---
      end
    } # self.note.split
    #---
  end
  
end # RPG::UsableItem

# ■ Game_Battler
#==============================================================================

class Game_Battler < Game_BattlerBase
  
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # alias method: item_user_effect
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  alias game_battler_item_user_effect_antifail item_user_effect
  def item_user_effect(user, item)
    game_battler_item_user_effect_antifail(user, item)
    apply_antifail(item)
  end
  
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # new method: apply_antifail
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  def apply_antifail(item)
    @result.success = true if item.antifail
    @result.suppress = true if item.antifail #Koda
  end
  
end # Game_Battler

============= SCRIPT 147: Koda - Battle Aesthetics =============

#==============================================================================
# ** Window_BattleLog
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
class Window_BattleLog < Window_Selectable
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # * Display Skill/Item Use
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  def display_use_item(subject, item)
    name = subject.name
    name = "You" if name == $game_actors[1].name
    
    if item.is_a?(RPG::Skill)
      unless item.message1.empty?
        add_text(name + item.message1)
        unless item.message2.empty?
          wait
          add_text(item.message2)
        end
      end
    else
      add_text(sprintf(Vocab::UseItem, name, item.name))
    end
  end
  
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # * Display Failure
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  def display_failure(target, item)
    if target.result.hit? && !target.result.success
      # Koda Disabled
      #add_text(sprintf(Vocab::ActionFailure, target.name))
      #wait
    end
  end
end


#==============================================================================
# ** Game_Actor
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
class Game_Actor < Game_Battler
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # * Show Added State
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  def show_added_states
    @result.added_state_objects.each do |state|
      $game_message.add(name + state.message1) unless state.message1.empty?
    end
  end
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # * Show Removed State
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  def show_removed_states
    @result.removed_state_objects.each do |state|
      $game_message.add(name + state.message4) unless state.message4.empty?
    end
  end
end

#==============================================================================
# ** Game_ActionResult
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
class Game_ActionResult
  def hp_damage_text
    btlname = @battler == $game_actors[1] ? "You" : @battler.name
    
    if @hp_drain > 0
      fmt = @battler.actor? ? Vocab::ActorDrain : Vocab::EnemyDrain
      sprintf(fmt, btlname, Vocab::hp, @hp_drain)
    elsif @hp_damage > 0
      fmt = @battler.actor? ? Vocab::ActorDamage : Vocab::EnemyDamage
      sprintf(fmt, btlname, @hp_damage)
    elsif @hp_damage < 0
      fmt = @battler.actor? ? Vocab::ActorRecovery : Vocab::EnemyRecovery
      sprintf(fmt, btlname, Vocab::hp, -hp_damage)
    else
      fmt = @battler.actor? ? Vocab::ActorNoDamage : Vocab::EnemyNoDamage
      sprintf(fmt, btlname)
    end
  end
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # * Get Text for MP Damage
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  def mp_damage_text
    btlname = @battler == $game_actors[1] ? "You" : @battler.name
    
    if @mp_drain > 0
      fmt = @battler.actor? ? Vocab::ActorDrain : Vocab::EnemyDrain
      sprintf(fmt, btlname, Vocab::mp, @mp_drain)
    elsif @mp_damage > 0
      fmt = @battler.actor? ? Vocab::ActorLoss : Vocab::EnemyLoss
      sprintf(fmt, btlname, Vocab::mp, @mp_damage)
    elsif @mp_damage < 0
      fmt = @battler.actor? ? Vocab::ActorRecovery : Vocab::EnemyRecovery
      sprintf(fmt, btlname, Vocab::mp, -@mp_damage)
    else
      ""
    end
  end
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # * Get Text for TP Damage
  #--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  def tp_damage_text
    btlname = @battler == $game_actors[1] ? "You" : @battler.name
    
    if @tp_damage > 0
      fmt = @battler.actor? ? Vocab::ActorLoss : Vocab::EnemyLoss
      sprintf(fmt, btlname, Vocab::tp, @tp_damage)
    elsif @tp_damage < 0
      fmt = @battler.actor? ? Vocab::ActorGain : Vocab::EnemyGain
      sprintf(fmt, btlname, Vocab::tp, -@tp_damage)
    else
      ""
    end
  end
end

class Game_Picture
  alias tint_over_pic_init_tone init_tone
  def init_tone
    tint_over_pic_init_tone
    
      if $game_party.in_battle
        @tone = tone_clone_amp
      else
        #Santa
        if Santa.workshop?
          if Santa.time == :night
            @tone = Tone.new(-36, -36, 0, 36)
          else
            @tone = Tone.new(5, -15, -30, 30)
          end
        end
        
        if $game_switches[141] #Switch On
          @tone = tone_clone_amp
        end
      end
  end
end
============= SCRIPT 149: Bowei - Zoom =============
# Map Effects v1.4.1 for VX and VXace by Zeus81
# 30€ for commercial use
# Licence : http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/
# Contact : zeusex81@gmail.com
# (fr) Manuel d'utilisation : https://www.dropbox.com/s/lb1d3q9jmx53taf/Map%20Effects%20Doc%20Fr.txt
# (en) User Guide           : https://www.dropbox.com/s/sk3uwq2bleoxr7s/Map%20Effects%20Doc%20En.txt
#      Demo : https://www.dropbox.com/s/2ex6906dyehl7an/Map%20Effects.zip

$imported ||= {}
$imported[:Zeus_Map_Effects] = __FILE__

def xp?() false end ; def vx?() false end ; def vxace?() false end
RUBY_VERSION == '1.8.1' ? defined?(Hangup) ?
def xp?() true  end : def vx?() true  end : def vxace?() true  end

class << Graphics
  def snap_elements_to_bitmap(*elements)
    if !@snap_elements_back or @snap_elements_back.disposed?
      @snap_elements_back = Sprite.new
      @snap_elements_back.bitmap = Bitmap.new(1, 1)
      @snap_elements_back.bitmap.set_pixel(0, 0, Color.new(0, 0, 0))
      @snap_elements_back.z = 0x0FFF_FFFF
    end
    @snap_elements_back.zoom_x = width
    @snap_elements_back.zoom_y = height
    @snap_elements_back.visible = true
    elements.each {|element| element.z += 0x1FFF_FFFF}
    bmp = snap_to_bitmap rescue retry
    @snap_elements_back.visible = false
    elements.each {|element| element.z -= 0x1FFF_FFFF}
    return bmp
  end
end

module Math
module_function
  def min(x, y) x < y ? x : y end
  def max(x, y) x < y ? y : x end
  def middle(min, x, max) x < max ? x < min ? min : x : max end
end

module Zeus
  module Animation
    def animate(variable, target_value, duration=0, ext=nil)
      @za_animations ||= {}
      base_value = Marshal.load(Marshal.dump(instance_variable_get(variable)))
      if duration < 1
        update_animation_value(variable, base_value, target_value, 1, 1, ext)
        @za_animations.delete(variable)
      else
        @za_animations[variable] = [base_value, target_value, 0, duration.to_i, ext]
      end
    end
    def animating?
      @za_animations and !@za_animations.empty?
    end
    def clear_animations
      @za_animations and @za_animations.clear
    end
    def memorize_animations(variables = instance_variables)
      data = {}
      variables.each {|var| data[var.to_sym] = instance_variable_get(var)}
      data.delete(:@za_memorize)
      @za_memorize = Marshal.dump(data)
    end
    def restore_animations
      return unless @za_memorize
      Marshal.load(@za_memorize).each {|var,value| instance_variable_set(var,value)}
    end
    def update_animations
      return unless @za_animations
      @za_animations.delete_if do |variable, data|
        data[2] += 1
        update_animation_value(variable, *data)
        data[2] == data[3]
      end
    end
  private
    def calculate_next_value(base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total)
      base_value + (target_value - base_value) * duration / duration_total
    end
    def update_animation_value(variable, base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total, ext)
      method_name = "update_animation_variable_#{variable.to_s[1..-1]}"
      method_name = "update_animation_#{base_value.class}" unless respond_to?(method_name)
      send(method_name, variable, base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total, ext)
    end
    def update_animation_Color(variable, base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total, ext)
      value = instance_variable_get(variable)
      value.red   = calculate_next_value(base_value.red  , target_value.red  , duration, duration_total)
      value.green = calculate_next_value(base_value.green, target_value.green, duration, duration_total)
      value.blue  = calculate_next_value(base_value.blue , target_value.blue , duration, duration_total)
      value.alpha = calculate_next_value(base_value.alpha, target_value.alpha, duration, duration_total)
    end
    def update_animation_Tone(variable, base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total, ext)
      value = instance_variable_get(variable)
      value.red   = calculate_next_value(base_value.red  , target_value.red  , duration, duration_total)
      value.green = calculate_next_value(base_value.green, target_value.green, duration, duration_total)
      value.blue  = calculate_next_value(base_value.blue , target_value.blue , duration, duration_total)
      value.gray  = calculate_next_value(base_value.gray , target_value.gray , duration, duration_total)
    end
    def update_animation_Float(variable, base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total, ext)
      value = calculate_next_value(base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total)
      instance_variable_set(variable, value)
    end
    alias update_animation_Fixnum update_animation_Float
    alias update_animation_Bignum update_animation_Float
  end
end

class Game_Map_Effects
  include Zeus::Animation
  attr_accessor :active, :refresh_rate, :back, :x, :y, :ox, :oy, :angle,
                :zoom_x, :zoom_y, :mirror, :opacity, :blend_type, :color, :tone,
                :hue, :wave_amp, :wave_length, :wave_speed, :wave_phase,
                :pixelize, :blur_division, :blur_fade, :blur_animation,
                :gaussian_blur_length, :linear_blur_angle, :linear_blur_length,
                :radial_blur_angle, :zoom_blur_length, :motion_blur_rate
  def initialize
    @active = true
    @refresh_rate = 30.0
    clear
  end
  def clear
    @back = false
    @x = @ox = Graphics.width  / 2
    @y = @oy = Graphics.height / 2
    @zoom_x = 1.0
    @zoom_y = 1.0
    @zoom2 = Math.sqrt(100.0)
    @angle = 0.0
    @wave_amp = 0.0
    @wave_length = 180
    @wave_speed = 360
    @wave_phase = 0.0
    @mirror = false
    @opacity = 255
    @blend_type = 0
    @color ||= Color.new(0, 0, 0, 0)
    @color.set(0, 0, 0, 0)
    @tone ||= Tone.new(0, 0, 0, 0)
    @tone.set(0, 0, 0, 0)
    @hue = 0
    @pixelize = 1.0
    @pixelize2 = Math.sqrt(100.0)
    @blur_division = 4.0
    @blur_fade = 1.0
    @blur_animation = 0.0
    @gaussian_blur_length = 0.0
    @linear_blur_angle = 0.0
    @linear_blur_length = 0.0
    @radial_blur_angle = 0.0
    @zoom_blur_length = 0.0
    @motion_blur_rate = 0.0
    clear_animations
  end
  alias memorize memorize_animations
  alias restore  restore_animations
  alias update   update_animations
  def active?
    return false unless @active
    animating? or blur? or @mirror or @blend_type != 0 or
    @zoom_x != 1 or @zoom_y != 1 or @pixelize > 1 or
    @angle % 360 != 0 or @hue.to_i % 360 != 0 or @color.alpha != 0 or
    @tone.red != 0 or @tone.green != 0 or @tone.blue != 0 or @tone.gray != 0 or
    (@wave_amp * @zoom_x >= 1 and @wave_length * @zoom_y >= 1)
  end
  def blur?
    return false if @blur_division < 1
    @gaussian_blur_length != 0 or @linear_blur_length != 0 or
    @radial_blur_angle != 0 or @zoom_blur_length != 0 or @motion_blur_rate != 0
  end
  def refresh_bitmap?
    @refresh_rate > 0 and
    Graphics.frame_count % (Graphics.frame_rate / @refresh_rate.to_f) < 1
  end
  def tilemap_wave_sync(tilemap_oy)
    return 0 if @wave_length == 0
    tilemap_oy * @wave_speed / @wave_length.to_f
  end
  def blur_animation_offset
    return 0 if @blur_animation == 0
    1 - @blur_animation * Graphics.frame_count / Graphics.frame_rate.to_f % 1
  end
  def refresh_motion_blur?
    @blur_division >= 1 and @motion_blur_rate > 0 and
    Graphics.frame_count % @motion_blur_rate < 1
  end
  def set_origin(x, y, duration=0)
    x = x * Graphics.width  / 100
    y = y * Graphics.height / 100
    animate(:@x , x, duration)
    animate(:@y , y, duration)
    animate(:@ox, x, duration)
    animate(:@oy, y, duration)
  end
  def set_zoom(zoom, duration=0, center_on_player=true)
    zoom = Math.sqrt(Math.max(1, zoom))
    animate(:@zoom2, zoom, duration, center_on_player)
  end
  def update_animation_variable_zoom2(variable, base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total, center_on_player)
    update_animation_Float(variable, base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total, nil)
    @zoom_y = @zoom_x = @zoom2 ** 2 / 100.0
    display_ratio = Game_Map::DisplayRatio.to_f
    if center_on_player
      x = $game_player.real_x / display_ratio
      y = $game_player.real_y / display_ratio
    else
      x = $game_map.display_x / display_ratio + $game_map.screen_tile_x / 2
      y = $game_map.display_y / display_ratio + $game_map.screen_tile_y / 2
    end
    $game_player.center(x, y)
  end
  def set_angle(angle, duration=0)
    animate(:@angle, angle, duration)
  end
  def set_opacity(opacity, duration=0)
    opacity = opacity * 255 / 100
    animate(:@opacity, opacity, duration)
  end
  def set_color(red, green, blue, alpha, duration=0)
    animate(:@color, Color.new(red, green, blue, alpha), duration)
  end
  def set_tone(red, green, blue, gray, duration=0)
    animate(:@tone, Tone.new(red, green, blue, gray), duration)
  end
  def set_hue(hue, duration=0)
    animate(:@hue, hue, duration)
  end
  def set_wave(amp, length, speed, duration=0)
    animate(:@wave_amp   , amp   , duration)
    animate(:@wave_length, length, duration)
    animate(:@wave_speed , speed , duration)
  end
  def set_pixelize(pixelize, duration=0)
    pixelize = Math.sqrt(Math.max(100, pixelize))
    animate(:@pixelize2, pixelize, duration)
  end
  def update_animation_variable_pixelize2(variable, base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total, ext)
    update_animation_Float(variable, base_value, target_value, duration, duration_total, ext)
    @pixelize  = @pixelize2 ** 2 / 100.0
  end
  def setup_blur(division, fade, animation, duration=0)
    division = Math.middle(0, division, 16)
    animate(:@blur_division , division , duration)
    animate(:@blur_fade     , fade     , duration)
    animate(:@blur_animation, animation, duration)
  end
  def set_gaussian_blur(length, duration=0)
    animate(:@gaussian_blur_length, length, duration)
  end
  def set_linear_blur(angle, length, duration=0)
    animate(:@linear_blur_angle , angle , duration)
    animate(:@linear_blur_length, length, duration)
  end
  def set_radial_blur(angle, duration=0)
    animate(:@radial_blur_angle, angle, duration)
  end
  def set_zoom_blur(zoom, duration=0)
    length = Math.max(1, zoom) / 100.0 - 1
    animate(:@zoom_blur_length, length, duration)
  end
  def set_motion_blur(rate, duration=0)
    animate(:@motion_blur_rate, rate, duration)
  end
end

class Spriteset_Map_Effects
  Blur_Offset = [[0.7,0.7], [-0.7,-0.7], [-0.7,0.7], [0.7,-0.7],
                 [0,1], [0,-1], [1,0], [-1,0]]
  def initialize(*viewports)
    @map_viewports    = viewports
    @viewport         = Viewport.new(viewports[0].rect)
    @viewport.z       = viewports[0].z
    @viewport.visible = false
    @effects_sprites  = []
    @effects_bitmaps  = []
    @data             = $game_map.effects
  end
  def dispose(dispose_viewport=true)
    @effects_sprites.each {|sprite| sprite.dispose}
    @effects_sprites.clear
    @effects_bitmaps.each {|bitmap| bitmap.dispose if bitmap}
    @effects_bitmaps.clear
    @pixelize_bitmap.dispose if @pixelize_bitmap
    @pixelize_bitmap = nil
    @back_sprite.dispose if @back_sprite
    @back_sprite = nil
    if dispose_viewport
      @viewport.dispose
    else
      @viewport.visible = false
      @map_viewports.each {|viewport| viewport.visible = true}
    end
  end
  def update(tilemap_oy = 0)
    unless @data.active?
      dispose(false) if @viewport.visible
      return
    end
    @viewport.visible = true
    @motion_blur_refresh ||= @data.refresh_motion_blur?
    refresh_sprites
    if !@effects_bitmaps[0] or @data.refresh_bitmap?
      refresh_bitmaps
      refresh_pixelize
    end
    refresh_back
    wave_sync   = @data.tilemap_wave_sync(tilemap_oy)
    blur_offset = @data.blur_animation_offset
    @effects_sprites.each_with_index do |sprite, id|
      update_effects(sprite, id, wave_sync)
      update_pixelize(sprite) if @pixelize_bitmap
      update_blur(sprite, id, blur_offset) if id > 0
    end
    @data.wave_phase = @effects_sprites[0].wave_phase - wave_sync
  end
  def refresh_sprites
    n = (@data.blur? ? @data.blur_division.to_i+1 : 1) - @effects_sprites.size
    n.times {@effects_sprites << Sprite.new(@viewport)}
    (-n).times {@effects_sprites.pop.dispose}
  end
  def refresh_bitmaps
    n = (@data.motion_blur_rate == 0 ? 1 : @effects_sprites.size) - @effects_bitmaps.size
    n.times {@effects_bitmaps << nil}
    (-n).times {bmp = @effects_bitmaps.pop and bmp.dispose}
    @map_viewports.each {|viewport| viewport.visible = true}
    @effects_bitmaps.unshift(@effects_bitmaps.pop) if @motion_blur_refresh
    @effects_bitmaps[0].dispose if @effects_bitmaps[0]
    @effects_bitmaps[0] = Graphics.snap_elements_to_bitmap(*@map_viewports)
    @effects_bitmaps[0].hue_change(@data.hue % 360) if @data.hue.to_i % 360 != 0
    @map_viewports.each {|viewport| viewport.visible = false}
